title: Sanity tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Sanity API token detected in source code. Sanity tokens provide access to your Sanity.io CMS content platform, granting the ability to read, write, and manage structured content, datasets, documents, and assets. Exposing these keys allows unauthorized users to: access and exfiltrate your entire content database (articles, pages, media), modify or delete published content causing website outages, inject malicious content and SEO spam, access unpublished drafts and confidential content, manipulate project configurations and user permissions, consume API quota leading to service disruption, and access customer/user data stored in your CMS. Store tokens securely using environment variables (SANITY_API_TOKEN) or secret management systems (AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, HashiCorp Vault). IMMEDIATELY revoke exposed tokens from manage.sanity.io project settings and rotate credentials. Enable IP whitelisting, use read-only tokens where possible, implement CORS policies, monitor API usage logs, and employ dataset access controls.

Sanity API token detected in Authorization header with Bearer format. This token grants comprehensive access to your Sanity.io headless CMS platform. Exposed bearer tokens enable attackers to: perform GROQ queries and extract entire content databases, create/update/delete documents and assets across all datasets, access unpublished content and drafts, manipulate project schemas and configurations, inject malicious content for phishing or SEO poisoning, consume your API rate limits causing service denial, access sensitive customer data stored in CMS, and bypass CORS restrictions in API authentication. CRITICAL: Immediately revoke this token from your Sanity project dashboard (manage.sanity.io → API settings → Tokens). Generate replacement tokens with minimum required permissions (read-only when possible). Implement secure storage using environment variables or secret managers (AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, Vault). Enable additional security measures: IP whitelisting for production tokens, CORS policy configuration, API usage monitoring and alerts, dataset-level access controls, token expiration policies, audit logging for all mutations, and separate tokens per deployment environment.
